Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Mutual Release
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 The Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 The Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for and with Joseph Yick.
Nicholas Campbell for Nick Yick and Katherine Yick.
Michael Freedman for Donna Yick.
Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick and Andy Ting.
Peter Meyers appeared by telephone for Tiffany Yick.

Mr. Campbell stated that Katherine has been appointed as Guardian Ad Litem in the Estate case and that she has the paper work to do it in the Trust. The Court stated that it will review the petition for appointment of Guardian Ad Litem when filed and that it will be granted subject to review of the order. There is no objection by anyone to the Court granting said petition. Mr. Conrad stated that he e-filed waiver of notice. Mr. Freedman stated that Donna has no issue. Mr. Koplowitz stated that Robert signed in both capacities and that part of the agreement is that the probate will be dismissed as it is moot. The Court questions signing the order for the probate matter when it is being dismissed. Counsel indicated that they want both orders (one for the Estate and one for the Trust) signed. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement and Mutual Release (subject to review of the order). The Court will make a determination as to whether it will sign both orders or not.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition To Require Trustee To Make Support Payments As Required By Trust
Off Calendar Jul-23-2018 Off Calendar
Ex Parte Application For Order Advancing Hearing On Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Continued To Aug-29-18 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 The Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Michael Freedman for Donna Yick (Petitioner).
Melissa Sanders for Tiffany Yick.
Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick.
Bruce Methven for Katherine Yick (Guardian Ad Litem for Nicholas Yick).
Edward Koplowitz for Joe Yick.

Mr. Methven stated that they had a number of objections to the proposed settlement, that they made some changes, stated some of the terms of the settlement, spoke of threats to litigate and to remove the Nicholas' Mother, stated that they was a settlement, but when it came back it was substantially different and worse for Nicholas, and that they removed the deadline to refinance. Mr. Koplowitz noted that everything the Court just heard is privileged, stated that two parties are financing this settlement, that they need clear titles in order to do the refinance, that everyone has worked really hard and spent a significant amount of time working on settlement to avoid a trial and that Judge Wiss has offered to do a settlement conference. Mr. Freedman stated that the petition to determine construction of trust can be tried in this department in a matter of hours, that it is a question of power of appointment, asked the Court to consider a motion to severe the petition on construction of trust and stated that the petition to determine validity of trust will be a much lengthy trial. Mr. Methven and Mr. Conrad stated that they would like to have a settlement with Judge Wiss. Mr. Conrad stated that substantial discovery will be needed, and that if Mr. Freedman files a motion to severe, he will oppose it. The Court set a hearing on the motion to severe for August 29, 2018 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also orders a settlement conference with Judge Wiss. Mr. Koplowitz will prepare an order. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust Instrument, etc. and the Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. (2) are also continued to August 29, 2018 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Continued To Aug-29-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application To Shorten Time On Hearing For Petition For Support Of Nicholas Yick
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Continued To May-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 The Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Joseph Yick.
Michael Freedman for Donna YIck.
Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick.
Peter Myers for Tiffany Yick-Whitney.

The Court asked for an update regarding mediation/settlement. Mr. Conrad stated that the person the Court needs to talk to is a fifth party Katherine Yick who is the Mother and Guardian Ad Litem for Nicky Yick (Minor), that Katherine is represented by Attorney Bruce Methven who is not present and that they attended eight mediations. Mr. Koplowitz also stated that the amount negotiated for the Minor was very generous, that he received a letter from Mr. Methven on April 18th, that he sent a letter back to Mr. Methven explaining what they can and can not do, that the alternative is to go to trial, expressed concern about the Minor's interest not being looked out for and stated that the Minor has no pending petition for relief. Mr. Freedman raised the issue of having the Court appoint a neutral Guardian Ad Litem for the Minor, and spoke of Trust history and Shew Yicks power of appointment. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust Instrument, etc. and the Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. (2) are continued to May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to Bruce Methven to appear on May 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Continued To Mar-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 The Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 The Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick.
Michael Freedman for Donna Yick.
Edward Koplowitz for Joseph Yick.
Peter Myers for Tiffany Yick.

Counsel reported that mediation went well, that there is still a dispute between Mr. Conrad's and Mr. Koplowitz's Clients, that doing partial settlement is not practical with the way the assets are set up and requested a continuance to continue working on global settlement. Mr. Freedman stated that he is satisfied with his pecuniary settlement and consents to an extension of time. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust Instrument, etc. and the Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. are continued to March 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Continued To Jan-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 The Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 the Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Ed Koplowitz for Joseph Yick.
Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick (Trustee).
Michael Freedman for Donna Yick.
Peter Myers appeared by telephone for Tiffany Yick-Whitney (Petitioner).

Mr. Koplowitz stated that they had successful mediation, that they have agreed on substance, that a settlement agreement is in circulation, that he is confident they can get it done within 30-days, requested a 45-day continuance and stated that they anticipate global settlement. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust, etc., and two Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. are continued to January 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-16-299442 The Estate of Shew Yick and PTR-16-299629 the Robert and Shew Yick Family Trust.

Appearances: Daniel Conrad for Robert Yick.
Lily Moallem for Tiffany Yick and special appearance for Attorney Michael Freedman on behalf of Donna Yick.

Mr. Conrad stated that there are three Trusts which own a piece of property, that the Trustees agreed to sell the property for payment of taxes, that the property was sold to the City for a substantial amount and that they are close to closing escrow. Mr. Conrad also stated that the Trustees agree to immediately pay all of the taxes and intend to return to settlement, that there is verbal agreement on terms, that they will do another round of mediation, and that he has spoken with Mr. Myers and Mr. Freedman and they all agree to continue the matter to August. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary, Petition to Determine Construction of Trust, etc., and two Petitions to Determine Validity of Purported Trust Amendments, etc. are continued to August 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of settlement and for selection of a hearing date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
